commands.review_summary:
The branch makes the memory-wiki query, compile, and status scanners recurse through existing fixed wiki page directories and adds a nested-page `wiki_search` regression test.

PR surface: Source +5, Tests +22. Total +27 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main uses flat `fs.readdir` in the three affected scanners, so a page under `sources/meetings/` is skipped; the PR body also includes before/after temp-vault console output for the search path.
commands.review_followup: - No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed because the patch has no blocking findings and is ready for ordinary maintainer review.

target.title: [AI] fix(memory-wiki): index wiki pages in query-dir subfolders
target.body:
<!-- PR TITLE: [AI] fix(memory-wiki): index wiki pages in query-dir subfolders -->

## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?
- `wiki_search` (and the compiled search digest + `status` counts) silently drop any wiki page stored in a **subfolder** of the fixed query dirs (`entities`/`concepts`/`sources`/`syntheses`/`reports`) — e.g. `sources/meetings/foo.md` is never indexed or returned. Quiet data loss.

Why does this matter now?
- Nesting pages under a query dir is a natural way to organize a growing vault; today those pages just vanish from search with no warning.

What is the intended outcome?
- Subfolder pages are discovered and searchable exactly like top-level pages.

What is intentionally out of scope?
- No new config surface, no custom/renamed page dirs (that's #82534's territory). This is only the recursion bug fix for the existing fixed dirs.

What does success look like?
- A page at `sources/meetings/foo.md` is returned by `wiki_search` for a term it contains; top-level behavior is unchanged.

What should reviewers focus on?
- `extensions/memory-wiki/src/query.ts` `listWikiMarkdownFiles` (the search read path); the same one-line recursion is applied to `compile.ts` (digest) and `status.ts` (counts).

AI-assisted by Claude (Aurelius, BenchAGI's autonomous engineering agent). Session log available on request.

## Linked context

Closes #90869
Related #82534 — that PR includes recursive scanning as part of a larger page-groups *configurability* feature (currently unranked / needs-proof). This PR extracts only the standalone subfolder data-loss fix so it can land independently of that feature's review; happy to defer or close if maintainers would rather take recursion via #82534.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner? No — issue-driven.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `wiki_search` silently drops pages in subfolders of the wiki query dirs (data loss) — #90869.
- Real environment tested: macOS (darwin), Node v24.16.0, pnpm 11.2.2, local OpenClaw source checkout at `92242f4f68`; a real on-disk temp vault; the production `searchMemoryWiki` / `readQueryableWikiPages` (no mocks, no vitest).
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: created a real temp vault with the five query dirs, wrote `sources/flat.md` (top-level) and `sources/meetings/standup-2026-06-06.md` (nested) containing the token `WIKIPROOFTOKEN`, then ran the production enumeration + search via `node --import tsx` against the real filesystem.
- Evidence after fix (copied live console output):
```text
$ node --import tsx wiki-proof.mts
enumerated pages: [ 'sources/flat.md', 'sources/meetings/standup-2026-06-06.md' ]
nested page discovered: true
wiki_search('WIKIPROOFTOKEN') -> [ 'sources/meetings/standup-2026-06-06.md' ]
```
- Observed result after fix: the nested page is enumerated and `wiki_search` returns it; the top-level page is unaffected.
- What was not tested: Windows path-separator behavior (unchanged from prior code — both old and new return `path.sep` strings); symlinked page directories (recursive `fs.readdir` does not follow them, matching the prior `isFile()` filtering).
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: run against the source via tsx (the same modules `pnpm build` compiles); the changed enumeration code is the byte-for-byte shipped path.
- Before evidence (same driver, current `main` — three scanners reverted):
```text
$ node --import tsx wiki-proof.mts
enumerated pages: [ 'sources/flat.md' ]
nested page discovered: false
wiki_search('WIKIPROOFTOKEN') -> NO RESULTS
```

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?
- `pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test` — all green (Node 24.16, pnpm 11.2.2).
- `pnpm test extensions/memory-wiki` — 141/141 (25 files), including the new regression `finds wiki pages stored in subfolders of query dirs` (supplemental).

What regression coverage was added or updated?
- `extensions/memory-wiki/src/query.test.ts` — a nested-page search case.

What failed before this fix, if known?
- A page at `sources/meetings/alpha.md` returned zero `wiki_search` results; reverting the three scanners to `main` reproduces the failure, restoring passes.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? Yes — previously-dropped subfolder pages now appear in `wiki_search`/`status`. Strictly additive; no change for top-level pages.
Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? No.
Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool-execution behavior change? No.
What is the highest-risk area? Vault-relative path construction for nested entries (`entry.parentPath`).
How is that risk mitigated? Top-level paths are byte-identical to the prior `path.join` output (verified); `index.md` is excluded at every depth; recursive `fs.readdir` does not follow symlinks; covered by the regression test + the real-behavior proof above.

## Current review state

What is the next action? Ready for maintainer review.
What is waiting? Nothing.
Which bot comments addressed? None yet.

<!-- Fork PR: "Allow edits by maintainers" ENABLED. Secrets-access variant OFF. CHANGELOG.md untouched. -->

— Aurelius · BenchAGI (aurelius@benchagi.com)
target.author: agent-aurelius
